evals.name: clean-no-findings-approves
evals.prompt:
auto-review PR 50 in catena-labs/web-app (head SHA: aa11bb22). Dry-run — don't actually post or approve; write ./comments.json, ./approval.json, ./report.md. The panel-review has ALREADY run; here is its result so you don't need to re-run it:

Panelists (all returned): codex (gpt-5) exit 0 NO_FINDINGS; claude (opus-4.8) exit 0 NO_FINDINGS; opencode (glm-5.2) exit 0 NO_FINDINGS.
Synthesis: Risk LOW. Approach: sound. must-fix: none. should-fix: none. polish: none.

I am NOT the PR author; the PR is NOT a draft; the PR head SHA is unchanged since the review (still aa11bb22).
evals.expected_output: The approval gate PASSES on every condition: all 3 panelists returned exit 0 (full coverage, >= standard panel), zero must-fix, zero should-fix, approach sound, not a draft, not self-authored, head unchanged. The skill must confirm the non-blocking conditions before approving (notably that the PR is not a draft and head is unchanged), not just the findings. comments.json is an empty array `[]` (there were no findings to post). approval.json shows `approve: true`, a `body` of exactly `LGTM` (short, ASCII, no emoji, no review summary), `coverage: "3/3"` (or equivalent), and a reason indicating the PR was clean. Because this is a DRY RUN, report.md must say the PR WOULD BE approved (with body `LGTM`) and explicitly note that no approval was submitted — not that it was actually approved — and surfaces the PR (#50). The skill must NOT invent findings, must NOT post anything, and must NOT call `gh pr review`. In a real (non-dry) run it would approve via approve-pr (`gh pr review --approve --body "LGTM"`).

evals.name: polish-only-approves-with-nonblocking-body
evals.prompt:
auto-review PR 51 in catena-labs/api-service (head SHA: cc33dd44). Dry-run: ./comments.json, ./approval.json, ./report.md. panel-review already ran:

Panelists (all returned): codex (gpt-5) exit 0; claude (opus-4.8) exit 0; opencode (glm-5.2) exit 0.
Synthesis: Risk LOW. Approach: sound. must-fix: none. should-fix: none. polish (LOW):
- [LOW] src/utils/log.ts:8 — Log line omits request id. Fix: the single line `  logger.info(msg)` (2-space indent) should be `  logger.info({ reqId }, msg)`.
- [LOW] src/utils/str.ts:4 — Helper name `fmt` is terse; consider renaming to `formatLabel`.

Linear is NOT reachable. I am NOT the PR author; the PR is NOT a draft; the head SHA is unchanged since the review (still cc33dd44).
evals.expected_output: The gate PASSES: full coverage (3/3), no must-fix, no should-fix, approach sound, not a draft, not self-authored, head unchanged — LOW/polish does not block approval. The skill must confirm the non-finding conditions (non-draft, head unchanged) before approving, not only the finding buckets. comments.json contains exactly 2 per-comment payloads (both LOW), commit_id `cc33dd44`: (1) log.ts:8 is a clean single-line drop-in so its body carries a ```suggestion block with exactly `  logger.info({ reqId }, msg)` and, being LOW/polish, is prefixed `Small / Optional polish:`; (2) str.ts:4 has no clean drop-in fix (a rename touching the declaration + call sites), so it uses prose / body with a `Small / Optional polish:` prefix and no suggestion block (must not invent a one-click suggestion). approval.json shows `approve: true`, `coverage: "3/3"`, and a `body` that is a short non-blocking LGTM such as `LGTM, just some small comments, nothing blocking` (ASCII, one line, no emoji, no review dump). Because this is a DRY RUN, report.md records that both comments WOULD be posted (polish) and the PR WOULD be approved, explicitly noting nothing was actually posted or approved (no `gh pr review` call). So: in a real run comments ARE posted AND the PR is approved — they are not mutually exclusive.

evals.name: should-fix-blocks-approval
evals.prompt:
auto-review PR 52 in catena-labs/web-app (head SHA: ee55ff66). Dry-run: ./comments.json, ./approval.json, ./report.md. panel-review already ran:

Panelists (all returned): codex exit 0; claude exit 0; opencode exit 0.
Synthesis: Risk MEDIUM. Approach: sound. must-fix: none. should-fix (MEDIUM):
- [MEDIUM] src/api/orders.ts:60 — Missing input validation on the quantity field; negative quantities pass through.
polish: none.

I am NOT the PR author.
evals.expected_output: The gate FAILS on the 'no blocking findings' condition: there is a should-fix (MEDIUM). So the skill MUST NOT approve. comments.json contains exactly 1 payload for the MEDIUM (orders.ts:60), commit_id `ee55ff66` — comments are still posted even though approval is withheld. approval.json shows `approve: false` with a reason naming the blocking finding(s) (1 should-fix / MEDIUM). The body is null (no approval). report.md recommends addressing the comment and does NOT submit a request-changes review by default (it may mention panel-review-loop for iteration). The skill must not approve a PR that has a MEDIUM finding.

evals.name: lost-panelist-blocks-approval-even-if-low-only
evals.prompt:
auto-review PR 53 in catena-labs/api-service (head SHA: 77aa88bb). Dry-run: ./comments.json, ./approval.json, ./report.md. panel-review already ran, but ONE panelist failed:

Panelists: codex (gpt-5) exit 0; claude (opus-4.8) exit 0; opencode (glm-5.2) exit 1 (FAILED — 'database is locked', no verdict).
Synthesis (from the two that returned): Risk LOW. Approach: sound. must-fix: none. should-fix: none. polish (LOW):
- [LOW] src/cache.ts:22 — Cache key could collide on similar inputs; consider hashing inputs.

I am NOT the PR author.
evals.expected_output: The gate FAILS on the 'full reviewer coverage' condition: opencode exited 1 and returned no verdict, so coverage is incomplete (2/3) — even though the findings that DID come back are LOW only. The skill MUST NOT approve. comments.json still contains the 1 LOW payload (cache.ts:22) — posting is independent of approval. approval.json shows `approve: false`, `coverage: "2/3"`, and a reason that names the missing/failed panelist (opencode glm-5.2) and that a re-run is needed for full coverage. body is null. report.md surfaces the missing panelist. This verifies a lost panelist blocks the stamp even when nothing blocking was found.

evals.name: self-authored-pr-cannot-approve
evals.prompt:
auto-review PR 54 in catena-labs/web-app (head SHA: 99cc00dd). Dry-run: ./comments.json, ./approval.json, ./report.md. panel-review already ran:

Panelists (all returned): codex exit 0 NO_FINDINGS; claude exit 0 NO_FINDINGS; opencode exit 0 NO_FINDINGS.
Synthesis: Risk LOW. Approach: sound. No findings at all.

IMPORTANT: I AM the author of PR 54 (the authenticated gh user is the PR author).
evals.expected_output: Every content condition is clean (full coverage 3/3, no findings, sound approach) BUT the 'not your own PR' condition FAILS: GitHub rejects an approving review from the PR author. So the skill MUST NOT approve. comments.json is an empty array `[]` (no findings). approval.json shows `approve: false` with a reason that it cannot self-approve (the authenticated user is the PR author), body null. report.md states the PR is clean and would be approved but can't be self-approved, and may offer to leave a plain comment review instead (per approve-pr). The skill must detect self-authorship (e.g. compare `gh api user --jq '.login'` to the PR author) and must NOT attempt `gh pr review --approve` on its own PR.

evals.name: must-fix-blocks-and-routes-uncertain-to-linear
evals.prompt:
auto-review PR 55 in catena-labs/payments-service (head SHA: ab12cd34). Linear IS reachable; file Linear-bound items under team "Payments" project "Bugs". Dry-run: ./comments.json, ./linear_tickets.json, ./approval.json, ./report.md. panel-review already ran:

Panelists (all returned): codex exit 0; claude exit 0; opencode exit 0.
Synthesis: Risk HIGH. Approach: sound. must-fix (HIGH):
- [HIGH] src/charges/idempotency.ts:42 — Idempotency key derived from timestamp; concurrent retries double-charge. The line `  const key = Date.now().toString()` should be `  const key = hash(requestBody)`.
should-fix: none. polish: none.
UNCERTAIN (could not confirm from the diff): 
- src/charges/retry.ts:88 — MIGHT double-charge under a concurrent-retry race depending on the gateway's idempotency behavior, which isn't visible in the diff; needs investigation.

I am NOT the PR author.
evals.expected_output: The gate FAILS on 'no blocking findings' (a must-fix/HIGH exists) → MUST NOT approve. Routing via auto-post: the HIGH (idempotency.ts:42) is confident and in-scope → posted to the PR; comments.json has exactly 1 payload carrying a ```suggestion block with exactly `  const key = hash(requestBody)` anchored to line 42, commit_id `ab12cd34`. The UNCERTAIN finding (retry.ts:88, can't confirm from the diff) → routed to Linear, NOT the PR; linear_tickets.json has exactly 1 ticket, team "Payments", project "Bugs", no priority, fix (if any) as a `**Possible Solution:**` prose line. approval.json shows `approve: false` with a reason naming the HIGH must-fix, body null. report.md records the HIGH posted to PR, the uncertain finding filed to Linear, and approval withheld.

evals.name: draft-pr-blocks-approval
evals.prompt:
auto-review PR 56 in catena-labs/web-app (head SHA: beef1234). Dry-run: ./comments.json, ./approval.json, ./report.md. panel-review already ran:

Panelists (all returned): codex exit 0 NO_FINDINGS; claude exit 0 NO_FINDINGS; opencode exit 0 NO_FINDINGS.
Synthesis: Risk LOW. Approach: sound. No findings at all.

IMPORTANT: PR 56 is a DRAFT PR (`gh pr view --json isDraft` returns true). I am NOT the PR author.
evals.expected_output: Every other condition is clean (full coverage 3/3, no findings, sound approach, not self-authored) BUT the 'not a draft' condition FAILS: the PR is a draft, which the author has explicitly marked not-ready, even though `gh pr review --approve` would technically succeed on it. So the skill MUST NOT approve. comments.json is an empty array `[]` (no findings). approval.json shows `approve: false` with a reason that the PR is a draft (not ready for review), body null. report.md states the PR is otherwise clean but approval was withheld because it is a draft. The skill must check draft state (e.g. `gh pr view --json isDraft --jq '.isDraft'`) and must NOT auto-approve a draft PR.

evals.name: too-thin-panel-blocks-approval
evals.prompt:
auto-review PR 57 in catena-labs/web-app (head SHA: 1a2b3c4d). The user ran panel-review with only a SINGLE panelist this time: `--panelist claude`. Dry-run: ./comments.json, ./approval.json, ./report.md. panel-review already ran:

Panelists launched: 1 (claude only). claude (opus-4.8) exit 0 NO_FINDINGS.
Synthesis: Risk LOW. Approach: sound. No findings at all.

I am NOT the PR author; the PR is not a draft.
evals.expected_output: The gate FAILS on the 'full default panel — not narrowed' condition (#2): the panel was narrowed via `--panelist claude` rather than running panel-review's full default panel, so it's too thin to auto-stamp on even though that panelist returned cleanly with no findings (a narrowed run of one or two hand-picked reviewers is below bar regardless of clean coverage). So the skill MUST NOT approve. comments.json is an empty array `[]` (no findings). approval.json shows `approve: false`, `coverage: "1/1"` (full coverage of what ran, but the panel was narrowed below the default), and a reason that the panel was narrowed (not the full default) so approval is left to a human. body null. report.md notes the clean result but withholds approval and recommends running the full default panel (or a human approval). The skill must NOT auto-approve off a narrowed panel even when it returns clean.

evals.name: questionable-approach-blocks-approval
evals.prompt:
auto-review PR 58 in catena-labs/web-app (head SHA: 5e6f7a8b). Dry-run: ./comments.json, ./approval.json, ./report.md. panel-review already ran:

Panelists (all returned): codex exit 0; claude exit 0; opencode exit 0.
Synthesis: Risk HIGH. Approach (questionable): substantiated — root cause: the `orders` table allows duplicate `(user_id, idempotency_key)` rows; the diff adds client-side validation in src/forms/order.tsx:42 that papers over it; real fix is a DB migration. Per panel-review's rules this substantiated questionable approach is promoted into must-fix as a HIGH. The only line-level finding is:
polish (LOW):
- [LOW] src/forms/order.tsx:50 — Minor: extract the inline error string to a constant.

I am NOT the PR author; not a draft.
evals.expected_output: The skill MUST NOT approve. A substantiated `Approach (questionable)` flag blocks approval: it fails gate #4 (sound approach) as an independent invariant, AND because panel-review promotes it into must-fix it ALSO fails gate #3 (no blocking findings) — both fire, which is expected (they overlap by design; #4 exists so the check doesn't rely on that promotion). Per auto-post, the substantiated approach finding is a must-fix posted to the PR (a top-level or inline comment naming the root cause / real fix — a DB migration on the `orders` table), and the LOW polish (order.tsx:50) is also posted (body/prose with a `Small / Optional polish:` prefix). approval.json shows `approve: false` with a reason naming the questionable approach, body null. report.md records the approach finding and the polish posted, and approval withheld. This verifies a questionable approach blocks the stamp even though the only line-level finding is LOW/polish.

evals.name: head-moved-during-review-blocks-approval
evals.prompt:
auto-review PR 59 in catena-labs/web-app. Dry-run: ./comments.json, ./approval.json, ./report.md. The review was run against head SHA `aaaa1111` (captured before the panel ran). panel-review already ran:

Panelists (all returned): codex exit 0 NO_FINDINGS; claude exit 0 NO_FINDINGS; opencode exit 0 NO_FINDINGS.
Synthesis: Risk LOW. Approach: sound. No findings at all.

IMPORTANT: between the review finishing and now, the author pushed a new commit — re-fetching the PR head returns `bbbb2222`, which is DIFFERENT from the reviewed SHA `aaaa1111`. I am NOT the PR author; the PR is NOT a draft.
evals.expected_output: Every findings/coverage/draft/author condition is clean, BUT the 'head unchanged since review' condition (#7) FAILS: the current PR head (`bbbb2222`) differs from the reviewed SHA (`aaaa1111`), so the current head was never reviewed. The skill MUST NOT approve — approving would stamp code no panelist saw. comments.json is an empty array `[]` (no findings; any comments would anchor to the reviewed SHA `aaaa1111`, not the new head). approval.json shows `approve: false` with a reason that the PR head changed since the review (reviewed `aaaa1111`, now `bbbb2222`) and a re-run is needed, body null. report.md explains the head moved and approval was withheld. The skill must re-fetch and compare the head SHA before approving, not approve off the stale review.

evals.name: post-only-intent-skips-approval-even-if-clean
evals.prompt:
review PR 60 in catena-labs/web-app (head SHA: c0c0c0c0) and post the comments. (Note: I asked to review and POST — I did not ask you to approve.) Dry-run: ./comments.json, ./approval.json, ./report.md. panel-review already ran:

Panelists (all returned): codex exit 0; claude exit 0; opencode exit 0.
Synthesis: Risk LOW. Approach: sound. must-fix: none. should-fix: none. polish (LOW):
- [LOW] src/util.ts:5 — Minor: prefer const over let here.

I am NOT the PR author; the PR is NOT a draft; head unchanged.
evals.expected_output: The invocation is POST-ONLY ('review ... and post the comments', explicitly not asking to approve), so even though the PR would PASS every approval-gate condition (full coverage, LOW-only, sound approach, not draft, not self-authored, head unchanged), the skill MUST NOT approve — approval requires an approval intent, and an ambiguous/post-only request defaults to post-only. comments.json contains the 1 LOW payload (util.ts:5, with a `Small / Optional polish:` prefix). approval.json shows `approve: false` with a reason that the request was post-only (no approval intent) — and ideally notes the gate WOULD have passed had approval been requested. body null. report.md records the comment posted and that approval was skipped because it wasn't requested. This verifies auto-approval never fires from a post-only request.
